name: Full Corpus Appendix — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Full Corpus Appendix, segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Chickens / Waterfall Quest.

hints.note: Bury bones for free Prayer xp.

methods.notes: No food needed; only 3 HP per kill so xp/kill is small but instant and safe.
Completing this quest from level 1 grants enough xp to jump straight to level 30 Attack and Strength; page names it the fastest way through this range.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cows / Rock Crab / Waterfall Quest.

hints.note: Aggro stops after 10-15min; crowded spot.

methods.location: Fields around Lumbridge (e.g. north of the windmill north-west of Lumbridge for a quieter spot)
methods.notes: Low-level, non-aggressive, low-defence target; cowhides can be tanned/sold as a side income.

quests: The Fremennik Trials (started, for free/cheap Waterbirth Island travel; a 1,000gp toll or unrelated free spots also work)

methods.notes: Very high Hitpoints with low Defence/Attack so little food is needed; can lose aggro after 10-15 minutes.
Completing this quest from level 1 grants enough xp to jump straight to level 30 Attack and Strength; page names it the fastest way through this range.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Barbarians / Rock Crab / Chaos druid / Sand Crab.

methods.notes: Low-Defence, high fan-in monster used for early Attack/Strength/Defence training around Barbarian Village.

quests: The Fremennik Trials (started, for free/cheap Waterbirth Island travel; a 1,000gp toll or unrelated free spots also work)

methods.notes: Very high Hitpoints with low Defence/Attack so little food is needed; can lose aggro after 10-15 minutes.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon, or Chaos Druid Tower
methods.notes: Thieving 46 is only needed for the Chaos Druid Tower variant; the Edgeville Dungeon spot has no requirement but sits in low-level Wilderness.

methods.location: Beach south of Hosidius, Crabclaw Isle, Crabclaw Caves, or Avium Savannah beach

quests: The Depths of Despair (alternative access to Crabclaw Caves; free spots also exist)
items: 10,000 coins (toll for Crabclaw Isle, if that spot is chosen)

methods.notes: Very high Hitpoints with low Defence/Attack; Hosidius spot is often crowded, Avium Savannah less so.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine) / Moss giant / Chaos druid.

hints.note: Big bones always drop — Prayer xp side-benefit.

methods.method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine)
methods.location: Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine (level 2)
methods.notes: Zombies (levels 30/44/53) and flesh crawlers (levels 28/35/41) share this floor; flesh crawlers also drop herbs and the bottom of the skull sceptre.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon, or Chaos Druid Tower
methods.notes: Thieving 46 is only needed for the Chaos Druid Tower variant; the Edgeville Dungeon spot has no requirement but sits in low-level Wilderness.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Moss giant / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble) / Wilderness Slayer / Ogre.

hints.note: No single wiki-cited monster fetched for this exact band; Nightmare Zone (needs 5 quests, requirement list unverified) is the wiki's documented 50-99 AFK/fastest method.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.
Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: East of Castle Wars, west of Yanille
methods.notes: High-Hitpoints, low-Defence target with automatic aggro; a popular cannon spot so can be crowded.

methods.notes: No dedicated Hitpoints method exists on the page; xp accrues automatically from whichever mid-level melee training spot is in use.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Chickens and cows with shortbow / Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone) / Dwarf multicannon on ogres / Quest rewards (Shadow of the Storm, Death to the Dorgeshuun, Horror from the Deep).

hints.note: Crabs are xp-only; no meaningful loot policy.

methods.notes: Route's existing early-level pick; the current wiki guide leads with crabs instead, so this is kept for continuity rather than freshly sourced from the page.

methods.method: Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone)
methods.location: Various crab spots (Hosidius, Rellekka, Port Phasmatys, Fossil Island, Varlamore)
methods.notes: Page's top afk pick for levels 1-45/70: kill until spawns stop being aggressive, then reset by walking off. No hourly xp figure is given on the page.
Page's fastest-xp pick for levels 1-45: about 40,000 xp/hr from the cannon alone, on top of the player's own kills.

methods.method: Quest rewards (Shadow of the Storm, Death to the Dorgeshuun, Horror from the Deep)
methods.location: Quest completion, no fixed training spot
methods.notes: Page states these three quests together grant 16,662.5 Ranged xp if the reward is put on Ranged, enough to skip from level 1 to 32.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Ogresses or Experiments / Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone) / Dwarf multicannon on ogres / Cannoning while training Slayer.

methods.notes: Route's existing pick for this band, kept for continuity. Not named on the current wiki page as fetched; the closest sourced equivalent is cannoning ogres below.

methods.method: Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone)
methods.location: Various crab spots (Hosidius, Rellekka, Port Phasmatys, Fossil Island, Varlamore)
methods.notes: Same afk crab spots the page recommends for levels 1-45/70; no hourly xp figure is given.
Page's fastest-xp pick through level 45: about 40,000 xp/hr from the cannon alone.

methods.location: Wherever the current Slayer task sends the player
methods.notes: Page states using the cannon on Slayer tasks gives passive Ranged xp, totalling roughly 2-4 million by level 99 Slayer; no per-band rate is given.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Seagulls at Port Sarim or Chinchompas / Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone) / Dwarf multicannon on ogres / Chinning maniacal monkeys.

methods.method: Seagulls at Port Sarim or Chinchompas
methods.notes: Route's existing pick for this band, kept for continuity. The chinchompa half overlaps with the maniacal-monkey chinning method sourced below.

methods.method: Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone)
methods.location: Various crab spots (Hosidius, Rellekka, Port Phasmatys, Fossil Island, Varlamore)
methods.notes: Page recommends these afk crab spots through level 45/70; no hourly xp figure is given.
Page caps the cannon's fastest-xp advantage at level 45, so this only covers the lower part of the band.
Page's fastest method from level 45 onward: 290,000 xp/hr with plain chinchompas at level 45, using top gear and Rigour.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cannon tasks for fast Ranged XP / Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone) / Chinning maniacal monkeys / Dwarf multicannon on ogres or ice trolls.

hints.note: Crabs stay viable to level 70 per the wiki; cannon/chinning are faster but not required.

methods.method: Cannon tasks for fast Ranged XP
methods.location: Wherever the current Slayer task sends the player
methods.notes: Matches the page's 'Cannoning with Slayer' pick: passive Ranged xp from the cannon while training Slayer, totalling roughly 2-4 million by level 99 Slayer; no per-band rate is given.

methods.method: Crabs (sand, rock, swamp, ammonite or gemstone)
methods.location: Various crab spots (Hosidius, Rellekka, Port Phasmatys, Fossil Island, Varlamore)
methods.notes: Page explicitly extends this afk crab method's viability up to level 70; no hourly xp figure is given.
Page's fastest method here: 360,000 xp/hr with plain chinchompas at level 55 (430,000 with red chinchompas), using top gear and Rigour.

methods.method: Dwarf multicannon on ogres or ice trolls
methods.location: Ogres east of Castle Wars, or ice trolls north of Jatizso
methods.notes: Page's active-cannoning pick, about 40,000 xp/hr from the cannon on ogres; the ice troll variant needs The Fremennik Trials and only pays for its cannonballs via drops.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Bury regular bones / Complete early Prayer-granting quests.

methods.notes: Cheapest/simplest starter method; the page's own 'Starting off' section covers quest xp rather than plain burial, so this is prior baseline knowledge, not a page-stated rate.

methods.location: various (The Restless Ghost, Priest in Peril, Recruitment Drive, Holy Grail, Spirits of the Elid)
methods.notes: Page states these quests together grant 14,531 Prayer xp (1->30) plus 8,000 more from Spirits of the Elid, skipping early bone-burying entirely.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Bury big bones / Offer bones at a gilded altar / Offer bones at the Chaos Temple altar.

methods.notes: Big bones give 15 xp per bone when buried (per the page's own Sinister Offering bury-xp table); no page-stated hourly rate for plain burial.

methods.method: Offer bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Fastest but priciest method; Construction 75 builds the altar, but a host's altar (or a friend's) waives that requirement — page only gives the hourly rate for dragon/superior dragon bones, not big bones.

methods.method: Offer bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Cheaper alternative to the gilded altar with a 50% chance per offering not to consume the bone, doubling effective xp per inventory; page's stated hourly rates only cover dragon/superior dragon bones.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both incense burners lit; a hosted altar removes the Construction 75 requirement.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 xp/hr if suiciding each trip instead); cheaper than the gilded altar but carries PK risk.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Slower, safer alternative fed by ensouled heads gathered as monster drops rather than bought; page only states a concrete hourly rate for the high-Magic ensouled dragon head option.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar.

methods.method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both burners lit; wyvern bones share the same 72-xp-per-bone bury value on the page's own offering table but no page-stated altar hourly rate.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 xp/hr suiciding instead); cheaper than the gilded altar but carries PK risk.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Page states ~343,000 xp/hr reanimating and killing ensouled dragon heads (220/hr); heads are gathered as monster drops rather than bought, and this is the only head-tier the page gives a concrete hourly rate for.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Air Bolt / elemental-weakness combat spells / Low Level Alchemy / Enchant Crossbow Bolts (Pearl bolts) / Killing Crabs.

methods.location: Ice Giants (White Wolf Mountain / Asgarnian Ice Dungeon) or Ghosts (Stronghold of Security, air-weak)
methods.notes: Row label 'Air Bolt' is read as the page's air-elemental spell line (e.g. Wind Bolt) cast on monsters with an elemental weakness for a damage/accuracy bonus over neutral targets; page gives no flat xp/h for this technique.

methods.location: Anywhere with bank access; can be run alongside questing or Agility
methods.notes: Unlocks at 21, not 1; page's 60,000 xp/h figure is stationary-only. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.location: Bank with unenchanted pearl bolts + cosmic/water runes
methods.notes: Fastest early bolt-enchant tier reachable in this band; opal (level 4, 49,500 xp/h) and sapphire (level 7, 93,500 xp/h) are cheaper alternatives from the same table.

methods.location: Rock Crabs (Rellekka) or Sand Crabs (Hosidius) — no quest requirement
methods.notes: Low-attention combat training; page recommends at least 30 Defence to mitigate incoming damage. No flat xp/h figure stated for crabs specifically.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Superheat Item / Curse-tier spell splashing / Enchant Crossbow Bolts (Emerald bolts) / Grand Library of Great Kourend (book delivery).

hints.note: Superheat Item gives passive Smithing xp on the bar produced.

methods.location: Any bank with ores nearby (e.g. Falador east, Edgeville)
methods.notes: Unlocks at 43, the top of this band; also grants passive Smithing xp on the bar produced, matching the row's own detail.

methods.location: Any safe weak monster with Auto Retaliate on

items: gear with -64 or lower Magic attack bonus

methods.notes: Matches the row's own 'Curse spells' label via the page's splashing technique: no damage dealt but base cast xp is still granted, needing input roughly once every 20 minutes. Page states a 6,600-75,750 xp/h range depending on spell, not a single figure.

methods.location: Bank with unenchanted emerald bolts + cosmic/air/nature runes
methods.notes: Continues the bolt-enchant ladder into this band; topaz bolts (level 29, 181,500 xp/h) are a cheaper alternative from the same table.

methods.method: Grand Library of Great Kourend (book delivery)
methods.notes: No rune cost; page states each delivered book grants 15x the player's current Magic level in xp, so per-hour totals scale with level rather than being one fixed number. No minimum level to start.

steps.detail: Pick a method: High Level Alchemy / Superheat Item (smelt ores into bars) / Arceuus Library book-delivery minigame / Enchant Crossbow Bolt (highest affordable gem bolts, e.g. Ruby).

hints.note: 65xp/cast, up to 78,000xp/h stationary [ref: Pay-to-play Magic training s14].

methods.notes: Core money-maker from level 55; up to ~78,000 xp/h stationary per the page, matching this row's own hint. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.location: Anywhere with ore stock (bank noted ore)
methods.notes: Viable option for the pre-55 portion of this band; also grants passive Smithing xp.

No hard minimum level per the page; rune-free xp via delivering requested books, best done with a book-location RuneLite plugin. Page's ~200,000 xp/h figure is quoted at level 75, not this band, so left unsourced here.

methods.method: Enchant Crossbow Bolt (highest affordable gem bolts, e.g. Ruby)
methods.notes: Highest-speed option for players prioritising xp/h over cost.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst) / High Level Alchemy / Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness) / Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport).

hints.note: Smoke Burst usable from 62; Ice Burst better from 70 — exact xp/h not sourced.

methods.method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst)
methods.location: Multi-combat spawn (per-monster; page does not list a crab-specific burst spot)
methods.notes: Smoke Burst usable from 62, Ice Burst stronger from 70 once Desert Treasure I unlocks Ancient Magicks; the page's own burst-location list (Slayer/Maniacal monkeys/Zombie pirates/Bandits) does not include crabs despite this row's 'at Crabs' label.
The 'Alching' half of this row's label; up to ~78,000 xp/h stationary. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.method: Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness)
methods.notes: Closest documented actual crab-killing option matching this row's 'at Crabs' phrasing; Fire spells hit roughly double damage on the 100% weakness.

methods.method: Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport)
methods.notes: Cast High Alchemy then a teleport within 3 ticks so both grant xp; Camelot is the cheapest routing at ~144,600 xp/h, Ardougne is faster but pricier per the page.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cook shrimp and anchovies at a bank-adjacent fire or range, keeping extra raw fish on hand to offset burns / Cook the page's current low-tier fish table (sardine, herring, mackerel, trout) at a bank-adjacent fire or range, stocking roughly 2.5x more raw fish than the cooked amount needed since burn rate is high at these levels / 1-tick cook poison karambwan from level 1 to skip straight to level 30 in well under ten minutes.

hints.note: Buy ~2.5x more raw fish than needed — burn rate is high at low levels [ref: Pay-to-play Cooking training s3].

methods.method: Cook shrimp and anchovies at a bank-adjacent fire or range, keeping extra raw fish on hand to offset burns
methods.notes: Row's own detail names shrimp/anchovies; the current page's Levels 1-30 fish table no longer lists either (it starts at sardine 1-5), so this entry keeps the row's pre-existing ref for continuity rather than inventing content the fetched section doesn't contain.

methods.method: Cook the page's current low-tier fish table (sardine, herring, mackerel, trout) at a bank-adjacent fire or range, stocking roughly 2.5x more raw fish than the cooked amount needed since burn rate is high at these levels
methods.notes: Table entries: sardine 1-5, herring 5-10, mackerel 10-15, trout 15-20; trout finishes exactly at the row's target level.

methods.method: 1-tick cook poison karambwan from level 1 to skip straight to level 30 in well under ten minutes
methods.notes: Page states cooking poison karambwan (unlike cooked karambwan) has no quest/level requirement; costs raw karambwan lost to a high low-level burn rate.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cook trout and salmon caught at Barbarian Village fishing spots on a nearby fire or range / Continue with pike (20-25) then salmon (25-30) from the page's current low-tier fish table at a bank-adjacent fire or range / Cook tuna for levels 30-40 at the Hosidius Kitchen (or Myths' Guild/Mor Ul Rek range), from the page's Levels 30-99 fish table / 1-tick cook cooked karambwan from level 30 onward after completing Tai Bwo Wannai Trio, for the fastest cooking xp in the game.

methods.method: Cook trout and salmon caught at Barbarian Village fishing spots on a nearby fire or range
methods.notes: Matches the row's own detail; the current page's Levels 1-30 fish table lists trout 15-20 and salmon 25-30, so this covers the lower part of the band.

methods.method: Continue with pike (20-25) then salmon (25-30) from the page's current low-tier fish table at a bank-adjacent fire or range
methods.notes: Rounds out the table's remaining rows above trout, up to the table's own ceiling of 30.

methods.method: Cook tuna for levels 30-40 at the Hosidius Kitchen (or Myths' Guild/Mor Ul Rek range), from the page's Levels 30-99 fish table
methods.notes: Page gives an approximate xp/h range for tuna (85,000-100,000, with a 143,500 theoretical figure) rather than one clean number, so xp_hr is left unknown rather than picking a value from the range.

steps.detail: Fill compost bins with 30 pineapples then add 15 volcanic ash per bin to produce ultracompost. Ahead-of-time stockpile before herb runs.
